deep_dive.executive_summary: At $57.23 (2026-07-17 close), the base scenario yields -8.4% per year over seven years on a partial commercial ramp path. AST SpaceMobile is a direct-to-cell satellite platform with $3.0 billion of cash, $2.96 billion of convertible debt, and 291 million diluted shares after repeated equity raises. FY2025 owner cash was deeply negative (-$3.42 per share) because capital spending of $1.06 billion dwarfed operating cash flow. Technical milestones with AT&T and Bell Canada de-risk the physics, but moat, dhando, and filing-based owner economics remain unproven at this price. Marvin proposes wat...

valuation.interpretation: value_per_share is withheld while any material additive component is unpriced; priced_components_per_share is not a complete security value.

scenario_contract.rule: Cases must change cited causal operating, capital, probability, timing, or financing drivers—not only a terminal multiple.

change_control.rule: A source fact is locked. Every assumption change requires a reason, author, timestamp, and before/after value.

options.label: Dated payoff (fallback)
options.tax_and_realization_adjustments: Risk and timing are reflected in the low/base/high range.
options.falsifier: Owner cash, reinvestment economics, or competitive position remains below the low-case path for two reporting periods.

optionality.rule: Every option requires a beneficiary, probability, timing, remaining capital, failure case, and non-overlap control; absence of option value must be explicit rather than unvalued.

committee.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

method_fit.label: Scarce assets, royalties, and optionality
applicability_tests: The security owns a legally enforceable economic claim on scarce assets or royalties.
Material value is omitted or poorly represented by current GAAP earnings.
Asset quantities, ownership, overlap, timing, and remaining capital can be reconciled.
Comparable values can be adjusted for location, quality, control, liquidity, and realization time.
failure_modes: gross asset value mistaken for shareholder value
operating cash flow and NAV counted twice
remote options treated as contracted cash

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: A complete security value is intentionally withheld while material components are unpriced. Attribution restarts after the first proof-complete baseline.

component_valuation.decision_rule: Dated-payoff fallback. Add an explicit component_valuation schedule before drawing asset-level conclusions.

valuation_proof.adjustment: Risk and timing are reflected in the low/base/high range.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Owner cash, reinvestment economics, or competitive position remains below the low-case path for two reporting periods.

total_return_panel.error: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.
total_return_panel.return_contract: split-adjusted close excluding dividends; event amounts normalized for later splits; cash reinvested at ex-date close; pre-tax nominal

coverage.note: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.

timeline.label: Long-term debt was not reported in the prior period and is now 2,207,583.0 (USD thousands).
Cash moved +313.4% versus the prior period (564,988.0 to 2,335,683.0).
Registered direct offering and concurrent private placement raised additional equity to fund Block 2 satellite manufacturing and launch cadence
First Block 2 BlueBird satellites launched; largest phased array deployed (~2,400 sq ft) versus Block 1 generation
Issued 2032 and 2036 convertible notes adding ~$2.2B long-term debt to fund constellation build while preserving cash runway
With Bell Canada, completed Canada's first space-based 4G VoLTE voice call, broadband data, and video streaming on unmodified smartphones
Block 1 BlueBird satellites launched; ten times higher throughput than BW3 test satellite per company disclosure
With AT&T, completed first VoLTE voice call and SMS over satellite using partner spectrum and standard unmodified phones
FY2024 10-K filed; total revenue $4.4M with early government services; net loss widened on constellation spending
Block 1 BB satellite launch marked transition from BW3 test program to operational BlueBird constellation build
FCC granted experimental and commercial licenses for SpaceMobile service testing in U.S. market
Completed SPAC merger with New Providence Acquisition Corp.; AST SpaceMobile listed on Nasdaq as ASTS
Formed strategic relationships with Vodafone, Rakuten and other MNOs to distribute SpaceMobile direct-to-cell service
Company founded to build space-based cellular broadband network using large phased-array satellites

industry.structure: Direct-to-cell satellite broadband is an oligopoly-in-formation: AST SpaceMobile, SpaceX/Starlink (D2C partnerships), Lynk, and legacy geostationary players compete for MNO revenue-share deals. Entry requires spectrum agreements, launch capacity, and phased-array manufacturing at scale.
industry.share_shift: ASTS is gaining technical credibility via AT&T and Bell Canada VoLTE milestones, but commercial revenue remains tiny versus capital deployed; Starlink and MNO-owned solutions press urgency on deployment timelines.
industry.trend: MNOs seek satellite fill-in for rural and emergency coverage without replacing terrestrial capex; Block 2 larger satellites and multi-launch cadence are the industry pivot from demo calls to revenue-bearing service.
